skill.name: receiving-code-review
skill.description: Guides AI coding agents to evaluate code review feedback before implementation, verify suggestions against the codebase, and respond with technical clarity.
skill.summary: Evaluate code review feedback before changing code.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown code fences as Ruby shell backticks and reported weak cryptography and network reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found no executable code, no cryptographic logic, and no network activity. The only real risk is a low-risk recommendation to use grep for local codebase verification.

low_findings.title: Local Search Command Recommendation
low_findings.description: The skill recommends using grep to inspect local code usage before accepting a review suggestion. This is a bounded local search workflow, not arbitrary command execution or data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 92 contains a direct grep recommendation, so the external command risk factor is real. The command is limited to local verification and does not include user-controlled shell construction.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Markdown Fence Execution Matches
low_findings.description: The static analyzer identified Markdown code fences and examples as Ruby shell backtick execution. These blocks describe review workflow steps and responses; they are not executable scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines are inside Markdown fenced examples in SKILL.md. No code file, shell script, or executable Ruby syntax is present.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Crypto And Network Matches
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography and network reconnaissance locations do not contain cryptographic algorithms or network scanning behavior. The lines are skill metadata, response examples, or communication guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the reported lines found no hash algorithms, ciphers, ports, hosts, or scanning commands. The scanner appears to have matched substrings in normal prose.

content.value_statement: Code review feedback can be unclear, incomplete, or wrong for the current codebase. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code verify feedback, ask clarifying questions, and implement changes carefully.
actual_capabilities: Defines a step-by-step process for receiving review feedback.
Encourages verification against codebase behavior before implementation.
Guides clarifying questions when feedback is incomplete.
Provides criteria for when to push back on review suggestions.
Suggests an implementation order for multi-item feedback.
Includes examples of concise technical responses.
limitations: Does not perform automated code review or static analysis.
Does not decide correctness without access to project context.
Does not provide language-specific refactoring rules.
Uses an opinionated communication style that may not fit every team.

use_cases.title: Handle Pull Request Feedback
use_cases.description: Use the workflow to verify review comments before changing code in a pull request.

use_cases.title: Guide AI Coding Agents
use_cases.description: Apply the response pattern when Claude, Codex, or Claude Code receives reviewer feedback.

use_cases.title: Resolve Conflicting Suggestions
use_cases.description: Use technical reasoning to identify unclear, risky, or context-dependent review requests.

prompt_templates.title: Review Feedback Safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the receiving-code-review skill. Read this feedback, restate what must change, and identify anything that needs clarification before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Verify Before Changing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the receiving-code-review skill. Check whether each review suggestion is correct for this codebase before making changes.

prompt_templates.title: Prioritize Multi-Item Feedback
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the receiving-code-review skill. Group these review items by risk and implementation order, then propose the first safe change.

prompt_templates.title: Challenge Risky Review Guidance
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the receiving-code-review skill. Evaluate this reviewer suggestion, find evidence for or against it, and draft a concise technical response.

output_examples.input: Reviewer says to remove a compatibility branch, but the target platform is unclear.
output_examples.output: The response identifies the compatibility question, checks project targets, and asks whether support for older platforms should remain.

output_examples.input: Reviewer requests six fixes, but two items are ambiguous.
output_examples.output: The response lists the understood items, names the unclear items, and pauses implementation until the missing details are resolved.

output_examples.input: Reviewer suggests adding a full metrics export feature.
output_examples.output: The response checks whether the endpoint is used, explains the evidence, and asks whether to remove unused code or implement the feature.

best_practices: Read all feedback before making changes.
Verify suggestions against tests, usage, and project constraints.
Implement one item at a time and test each change.
anti_patterns: Accepting review feedback without technical verification.
Implementing partial feedback when related items are unclear.
Adding unused features only because they sound complete.

faq.question: Does this skill write code changes automatically?
faq.answer: No. It guides how to evaluate feedback before code changes are made.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No evidence of network access was found in the skill file.

faq.question: Why does it mention grep?
faq.answer: It suggests grep as a local search tool to verify whether reviewed code is used.

faq.question: Is this a code review replacement?
faq.answer: No. It is a response workflow for handling feedback from reviewers.

faq.question: What is the main safety benefit?
faq.answer: It reduces blind implementation by requiring clarification and verification before changes.
